title: Speaking: Giving Instructions
learningGoal: Engage in a roleplay where you provide directions and polite instructions to a guest in your house.
instructions: You are hosting an elder guest at your house. You need to politely invite them in, tell them where to sit, and offer them food and water.
speakingPrompt: Respond to the prompts using polite Hindi commands ending in '-iye'. Ensure you use correct postpositions for locations.
scenario: Hosting a formal guest at your home.

subTasks.prompt: The guest arrives. Greet them and say: 'Please come inside.' (Come = Aa)
Offer them a seat. Say: 'Please sit here.' (Sit = Baith, Here = Yahaan)
Offer them water. Point to the table and say: 'Water is on the table.'
Offer them food. Say: 'Please eat food.' (Eat = Kha)

acceptableParaphrases: Saying 'Kripya andar aaiye' (Please come inside) is also excellent.

criteria.name: Formal Imperative Use
criteria.description: Must use 'aaiye', 'baithiye', and 'khaaiye'. Using 'aao' or 'khaao' will fail the task because the guest is an elder.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Namaste. Andar aaiye. Yahaan baithiye. Paani mez par hai. Khaana khaaiye.

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic hospitality! You spoke very politely and clearly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the formal commands. You must add '-iye' to the verb root when speaking to an elder.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Pay attention to your postposition order: object comes first, then the position (Mez par).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: नमस्ते। अंदर आइये। यहाँ बैठिये। पानी मेज़ पर है। खाना खाइये।

hintText: Speak with a welcoming tone. 'Khaaiye' rhymes with 'Aaiye'.
commonMistakes: Using informal commands like 'Aao' or 'Khaao' with the elder guest.
